trip.name: Phuket Odyssey 2026
trip.destination: Trang, Krabi, Phuket and Hat Yai, Thailand
trip.source: Built from supplied Phuket Odyssey 2026 itinerary screenshot, with free-time details filled in by Tripva.

days.title: Trang Arrival and Group Dinner
days.subtitle: Check in, settle down, and start the trip gently

timeline.title: Self check-in at Rua Rasada Hotel, Trang
timeline.detail: Original anchor from the event itinerary. Arrive independently, check in, collect room keys, and confirm the next morning convoy meeting point with the group.

timeline.title: Rest block and trip setup
timeline.detail: Use this window for shower, SIM/data top-up, cash withdrawal, pharmacy run, and fuel/vehicle checks before the Phuket convoy day.

timeline.title: Optional short Trang town coffee walk
timeline.detail: If energy is good, do a light town walk near the hotel area for coffee or local snacks. Keep it low-effort because Day 2 has a long drive.

timeline.title: Dinner at Thumrin Thana Hotel Restaurant
timeline.detail: Original anchor from the event itinerary. Group dinner and briefing. Confirm convoy departure time, route, rest stops, and rooming details for Phuket.

timeline.title: Early night or light supper
timeline.detail: Keep the night simple. Prepare water, snacks, power bank, sunglasses, and a small day bag for the drive to Krabi and Phuket.

localTips: Keep passport, license, insurance, and hotel confirmation in one pouch.
Buy water and motion-sickness tablets tonight if anyone gets car sick.
Set alarms early; the convoy leaves at 08:00 on Day 2.

days.title: Trang to Phuket via Krabi
days.subtitle: Convoy transfer, shopping stop, resort arrival, Patong evening

timeline.title: Breakfast, checkout, and convoy briefing
timeline.detail: Load luggage, check fuel, distribute route pins, and agree on lead car, tail car, and emergency contact. Keep all cars ready before 08:00.

timeline.title: Convoy leaves for Phuket
timeline.detail: Original anchor from the event itinerary. Drive from Trang toward Krabi first, then continue onward to Phuket. Keep one short restroom/fuel stop if needed.
timeline.mapQuery: Trang to Krabi to Phuket, Thailand

timeline.title: Central Krabi shopping and lunch stop
timeline.detail: Original anchor from the event itinerary. Good window for restroom, lunch, snacks, pharmacy, coffee, and a short mall walk before the final leg to Phuket.

timeline.title: Convoy proceeds to The Kee Resort, Phuket
timeline.detail: Original anchor from the event itinerary. Expect island traffic near Patong. Keep the group in convoy but do not rush the last stretch.
timeline.mapQuery: Central Krabi to The Kee Resort & Spa, Patong, Phuket

timeline.title: Arrive at The Kee Resort, Phuket
timeline.detail: Original anchor from the event itinerary. Check in, unload luggage, inspect rooms, and regroup in the lobby once everyone is settled.
timeline.mapQuery: The Kee Resort & Spa, Patong, Phuket, Thailand

timeline.title: Easy Patong orientation walk
timeline.detail: Walk the area around the resort and Patong Beach to understand where food, convenience stores, massage shops, money exchange, and pickup points are.

timeline.title: Photospot: Patong Beach sunset
timeline.detail: Simple first-night photo stop. Aim for beach silhouettes, resort skyline, and group shots before dinner.
timeline.mapQuery: Patong Beach sunset viewpoint, Phuket, Thailand
timeline.tip: Stand near the beach path and shoot west-facing frames before the sky gets dark.

timeline.title: Dinner near Bangla Road or Jungceylon
timeline.detail: Choose easy group dining: Thai seafood, hotpot, or mall restaurant if rain hits. Keep it close to the resort so people can split into nightlife/rest groups after dinner.

timeline.title: Free and easy: Patong nightlife or recovery
timeline.detail: Original free/easy window filled in. Option A: Bangla Road for the first nightlife taste. Option B: Thai massage and sleep. Option C: resort rooftop/drinks if the group wants something easier.

localTips: Traffic into Patong can slow down sharply in late afternoon.
Keep the first Phuket night flexible; people will have different energy after the long drive.
Use the hotel lobby as the fixed regroup point.

days.title: Phuket Highlights and Grand Dinner
days.subtitle: Old Town, viewpoints, food, buffet dinner, karaoke, and rooftop after-party

timeline.title: Breakfast and choose your day route
timeline.detail: Original itinerary says visit places of interest on your own. Recommended route: Phuket Old Town in the morning, Wat Chalong or viewpoint mid-day, Karon/Kata sunset, then back for dinner.
timeline.mapQuery: The Kee Resort & Spa, Patong, Phuket, Thailand

timeline.title: Photospot: Phuket Old Town heritage walk
timeline.detail: Walk Thalang Road and nearby Sino-Portuguese streets for cafes, murals, boutique shops, and colorful shophouse photos. This is the cleanest low-stress morning option.
timeline.mapQuery: Thalang Road, Phuket Old Town, Thailand
timeline.tip: Shoot the pastel shophouses, archways, wall murals, and cafe fronts before lunch crowds arrive.

timeline.title: Photospot: Soi Romanee and Old Town shophouses
timeline.detail: Best for colorful street photos and group portraits. Keep lens wide and avoid blocking shop entrances.
timeline.mapQuery: Soi Romanee, Phuket Old Town, Thailand
timeline.tip: Shoot from the opposite side of the lane to capture the pastel facades and lanterns.

timeline.title: Lunch in Phuket Old Town
timeline.detail: Pick a local Phuket-style meal: mee Hokkien, roti, crab curry, dim sum, or a cafe if the group wants air-conditioning.

timeline.title: Photospot: Wat Chalong temple stop
timeline.detail: A calm cultural stop before returning toward the beaches. Dress respectfully with shoulders/knees covered or bring a light cover-up.
timeline.tip: Use wide shots for the temple complex and tighter vertical frames for roof details and stair symmetry.

timeline.title: Optional Big Buddha viewpoint check
timeline.detail: Big Buddha status has been inconsistent after past closures. If it is confirmed open on the day, visit briefly. If not, skip it and use Karon Viewpoint instead.
timeline.mapQuery: Big Buddha Phuket, Karon, Phuket, Thailand

timeline.title: Photospot: Karon Viewpoint
timeline.detail: Safer default viewpoint stop with a strong view over Kata Noi, Kata, and Karon beaches. Good backup if Big Buddha access is restricted.
timeline.tip: Use 0.5x/wide camera for the three-beach view; keep people to one side of the frame.

timeline.title: Return to resort and reset
timeline.detail: Shower, change for dinner, recharge phones, and get ready for the ballroom event.
timeline.mapQuery: The Kee Resort & Spa, Patong, Phuket, Thailand

timeline.title: Grand dinner: buffet, free-flow beer, karaoke and dance
timeline.detail: Original anchor from the event itinerary. Ballroom dinner with international buffet, party, karaoke, and dancing.
timeline.mapQuery: The Kee Resort & Spa ballroom, Patong, Phuket, Thailand

timeline.title: After-party at Rooftop Bar
timeline.detail: Original anchor from the event itinerary. Continue at Rooftop Bar for anyone still able. Others can call it a night.
timeline.mapQuery: The Kee Sky Lounge Rooftop Bar, Patong, Phuket, Thailand

localTips: Keep Day 3 flexible: the dinner is the immovable anchor.
Check same-day weather before committing to viewpoint stops.
If Big Buddha access looks uncertain, do not waste time there; Karon Viewpoint is the better fallback.

days.title: Kata, Karon and Rawai Beach Day
days.subtitle: Free and easy coastal drive with beaches, seafood, and sunset

timeline.title: Slow breakfast and beach-day prep
timeline.detail: Original itinerary marks this as a free and easy day. Pack swimwear, towel, sandals, sunscreen, waterproof pouch, and a change of clothes.
timeline.mapQuery: The Kee Resort & Spa, Patong, Phuket, Thailand

timeline.title: Drive to Karon Beach
timeline.detail: Start with Karon because it is spacious and easier for a relaxed beach walk. Keep swim decisions weather-dependent.

timeline.title: Karon Beach walk and photos
timeline.detail: Long beach walk, coconut stop, and casual photos. Avoid swimming if red flags or rough surf are present.

timeline.title: Lunch around Kata or Karon
timeline.detail: Choose Thai seafood, noodle shop, or an air-conditioned cafe. Keep lunch simple so the group can continue south without dragging.

timeline.title: Photospot: Kata Beach free time
timeline.detail: Beach, cafes, massage, shopping, or rest. This is the main open relaxation block of the trip.
timeline.tip: Shoot beach layers: foreground umbrellas or palms, swimmers/surf, and the bay curve in the background.

timeline.title: Rawai seafood and coastal drive
timeline.detail: Drive south to Rawai for seafood atmosphere and local coastal views. Good option if the group wants more food than beach.

timeline.title: Photospot: Promthep Cape or Windmill Viewpoint
timeline.detail: Promthep is the classic choice; Windmill is often easier and less chaotic. Pick one based on traffic and parking.
timeline.tip: Arrive before sunset, secure parking, and shoot both sea horizon and group silhouettes.

timeline.title: Dinner at Rawai or return to Patong
timeline.detail: If everyone is hungry, eat seafood near Rawai. If the group wants nightlife or rest, return to Patong and eat near the resort.

timeline.title: Free evening
timeline.detail: Last proper Phuket night. Choose low-key massage, rooftop drink, Bangla Road, or early sleep before the Hat Yai transfer.

localTips: The event itinerary specifically recommends Kata, Karon, and Rawai; this route follows that direction.
July is rainy season, so keep beach stops flexible and watch surf flags.
Do not overpack the day; the best version is relaxed, not rushed.

days.title: Phuket to Hat Yai
days.subtitle: Convoy transfer, Regency Hotel check-in, and Lee Garden night market

timeline.title: Breakfast, checkout and luggage load
timeline.detail: Settle room bills, check all luggage, refill water, and prepare for the long convoy to Hat Yai.
timeline.mapQuery: The Kee Resort & Spa, Patong, Phuket, Thailand

timeline.title: Convoy proceeds to Hat Yai
timeline.detail: Original anchor from the event itinerary. Lunch is planned on board, so prepare snacks/drinks and keep the group driving rhythm steady.

timeline.title: Rest/fuel stop
timeline.detail: Recommended comfort stop. Stretch, use restrooms, rotate drivers if needed, and confirm ETA.
timeline.mapQuery: Phuket to Hat Yai route, Thailand

timeline.title: Lunch on board
timeline.detail: Original anchor from the event itinerary. Keep lunch light and convenient so the convoy does not lose time.
timeline.mapQuery: Phuket to Hat Yai route, Thailand

timeline.title: Optional coffee/restroom stop before Hat Yai
timeline.detail: Add one practical stop before entering town, especially if the group is tired or traffic builds.

timeline.title: Arrive at Regency Hotel Hat Yai
timeline.detail: Original anchor from the event itinerary. Check in, unload, refresh, and agree on a night-market meetup time.
timeline.mapQuery: Regency Hotel Hatyai, Hat Yai, Thailand

timeline.title: Photospot: Lee Garden night market and dinner
timeline.detail: Free and easy Hat Yai evening filled in. Walk the Lee Garden zone for street food, grilled seafood, mango sticky rice, Thai milk tea, fried chicken, snacks, and souvenir shopping.
timeline.mapQuery: Lee Garden Plaza, Hat Yai, Thailand
timeline.tip: Shoot neon signs, food stalls, and group candid shots before sitting down for dinner.

timeline.title: Kim Yong Market area or massage
timeline.detail: If the group wants more shopping, walk nearby market streets. If tired from the drive, choose foot massage and return early.
timeline.mapQuery: Kim Yong Market, Hat Yai, Thailand

timeline.title: Pack for border/home return
timeline.detail: Prepare documents, car items, luggage, and snacks for the independent return to Malaysia tomorrow.
timeline.mapQuery: Regency Hotel Hatyai, Hat Yai, Thailand

localTips: Keep Thai baht cash for markets and small food stalls.
Lee Garden zone is convenient for food and shopping near many central hotels.
Do final packing tonight to avoid morning stress.

days.title: Hat Yai Break and Return to Malaysia
days.subtitle: Final breakfast, optional market stop, and independent convoy break

timeline.title: Breakfast near hotel
timeline.detail: Easy final breakfast. Good options: hotel breakfast, local dim sum, kopi, or simple noodles before the drive home.
timeline.mapQuery: Hat Yai breakfast near Regency Hotel, Thailand

timeline.title: Optional final shopping: Kim Yong Market
timeline.detail: Pick up snacks, dry goods, souvenirs, and last-minute items. Keep this short if border timing matters.
timeline.mapQuery: Kim Yong Market, Hat Yai, Thailand

timeline.title: Checkout and convoy break
timeline.detail: Original anchor from the event itinerary. Convoy breaks here and everyone proceeds back to Malaysia on their own.
timeline.mapQuery: Regency Hotel Hatyai, Hat Yai, Thailand

timeline.title: Return to Malaysia
timeline.detail: Drive independently toward your preferred border crossing and final home destination. Confirm fuel, passports, vehicle documents, and snacks before leaving Hat Yai.

timeline.title: Lunch stop if needed
timeline.detail: Add a flexible lunch/rest stop depending on border queue and driver energy. Do not rush if tired.

timeline.title: Trip wrap
timeline.detail: Share photos, fuel/toll receipts, and group memories. Bye bye and see you again in the next trip.

localTips: Check border queue updates before leaving Hat Yai.
Keep passports and vehicle documents accessible, not buried in luggage.
Drivers should rest before the long return.

hotels.name: The Kee Resort & Spa
hotels.note: Main Phuket stay from 4 July to 7 July.

hotels.name: Regency Hotel Hatyai
hotels.note: Final night in Hat Yai on 7 July.

budget.note: Trang -> Krabi -> Phuket -> Hat Yai -> Malaysia convoy costs.
Lunches, casual dinners, coffee, snacks, and market food.
Beach activities, massage, viewpoints, shopping, and nightlife are optional.
Central Krabi, Phuket Old Town, Patong/Jungceylon, and Hat Yai markets.

urgent.label: Confirm convoy route and meeting points
urgent.note: Before 3 July, confirm lead car, tail car, emergency contacts, fuel/rest stops, and WhatsApp location-sharing rules.

urgent.label: Confirm hotel rooming list
urgent.note: Rua Rasada, The Kee Resort, and Regency Hotel Hatyai should all match the organiser list.

urgent.label: Check Phuket weather and sea conditions
urgent.note: July is rainy season. Adjust beach/swim plans if rain, rough surf, or red flags appear.

urgent.label: Verify Big Buddha status before visiting
urgent.note: The site has had reopening/closure confusion after landslide-related closures. Use Karon Viewpoint as the safe backup.

urgent.label: Prepare border documents for return
urgent.note: Passports, vehicle documents, insurance, cash, and driver rest before Hat Yai -> Malaysia.
